documentation.value: Prints the arguments followed by a newline. If no IO object is given, prints to stdout.
Optional IO stream to print to. Defaults to stdout.
Values to print.
Parses a string as the given type. For integers, an optional base can be specified.
The type to parse the string as (e.g., Int, Float64).
The string to parse.
Optional base for integer parsing (default 10).
Returns the concrete type of the argument.
The value whose type to determine.
Returns the number of elements in a collection (array, string, tuple, etc.).
The collection to measure.
Inserts one or more items at the end of a collection. Modifies the collection in place.
The mutable collection to append to.
Items to add to the end.
Removes and returns the last item from a collection.
The mutable collection to remove from.
Applies function f to each element of the collection(s) and returns an array of results.
The function to apply to each element.
One or more collections to map over.
Returns an array containing only elements of the collection for which f returns true.
A predicate function returning a boolean.
The collection to filter.
Reduces the collection using the binary operator op. An optional initial value can be provided.
A binary function or operator (e.g., +, *, min).
The collection to reduce.
Optional initial value for the reduction.
Returns a sorted copy of the vector. Use sort! for in-place sorting.
The vector to sort.
A function to transform elements before comparison.
If true, sort in reverse order.
Sorting algorithm to use (e.g., QuickSort, MergeSort).
Opens a file and returns an IOStream. Mode can be "r" (read), "w" (write), "a" (append), etc.
The path to the file to open.
File open mode: "r", "w", "a", "r+", "w+", "a+".
Reads a single value of the specified type from the IO stream.
The IO stream to read from.
The type of value to read.
Writes the canonical binary representation of the values to the IO stream. Returns number of bytes written.
The IO stream to write to.
The values to write.
Creates an array of all zeros with the specified element type and dimensions.
Element type (e.g., Float64, Int). Defaults to Float64.
Dimensions of the array (e.g., 3, 4 for a 3×4 matrix).
Creates an array of all ones with the specified element type and dimensions.
Element type (e.g., Float64, Int). Defaults to Float64.
Dimensions of the array.
